description: Daisy talks privately with Kora, questioning her loyalty and rationale for turning on Malick. The sisters' conversation gets personal as they each reflect on their childhood and feelings towards their mother, Jiaying. Kora expresses hope for a partnership, while Daisy reveals she is motivated by saving Simmons, not Kora. Sibyl’s prediction—that Daisy won’t let her sister fight alone—pushes Daisy toward her next decision.

transcript.line: We're not morons. Despite whatever Nathaniel has told you.
Nathaniel thinks S.H.I.E.L.D. is another institution hell-bent on control. And maybe in an old timeline it was, but... I believe there's a chance we could work together.
What makes you think that we would ever...
You. I've always wanted a sibling, and we have the same blood. You believe in S.H.I.E.L.D. There must be a reason.
I wasn't aware I was confined. I thought I had a happy childhood.
Not every mother loves her back.
I'm sorry you didn't get that.
I'm sorry you threw it away.
I just want to spend time together, to talk. With you and me on the same page, we can do anything.
What, like a... like a sister superhero squad?
Sibyl said there is no future where Daisy Johnson lets her sister fight alone.
